You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/nmstate-console-plugin/source/src/views/states/list/components/InterfaceDrawer/InterfaceDrawerDetailsTab.tsx

Apply ALL of the following 2 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Lines: 59, 73, 93
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-listitem]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <ListItem> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <ListItem> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <ListItem variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></ListItem>
  After:  <ListItem variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <ListItem> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <ListItem><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</ListItem>
  After:  <ListItem icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</ListItem>

Incident context:
  componentName: ListItem
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: List

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: ListItem
Prop: icon

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Code contexts:
```
  (line 59):
   58                {selectedInterface.lldp?.neighbors?.map((neighbor) => (
   59                  <ListItem key={getSystemName(neighbor)}>
   60                    <NeighborInformations neighbor={neighbor} />

  (line 73):
   72              {ports?.map((port) => (
   73                <ListItem key={port}>{port}</ListItem>
   74              ))}

  (line 93):
   92                ?.map((feature) => (
   93                  <ListItem key={feature}>
   94                    <Flex>

```

### Fix 2
Lines: 31, 49, 69, 80, 87
Rule [sd-new-absorbing-prop-stackitem-component]:
Component <StackItem> has a new prop `component` (type: `React.ReactNode`) added in v6 that accepts JSX content.

This prop may be intended to receive content that was previously passed as children. Review the JSX children of <StackItem> and determine if any should be moved to the `component` prop.

Example migration:
  Before: <StackItem>{content} other children</StackItem>
  After:  <StackItem component=>other children</StackItem>

Incident context:
  componentName: StackItem
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: Stack

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: StackItem
Prop: component
To: React.ReactNode

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

```

If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.

content.text:
Now let me analyze each fix:

### Fix 1 Analysis: `ListItem` icon-to-prop migration (lines 59, 73, 93)

**Line 59:** `<ListItem key={getSystemName(neighbor)}>` with child `<NeighborInformations neighbor={neighbor} />`. `NeighborInformations` is NOT an icon from `@patternfly/react-icons` — it's a custom component. No change needed here.

**Line 73:** `<ListItem key={port}>{port}</ListItem>` — the child is just text (`port` variable). No icon present. No change needed.

**Line 93:** `<ListItem key={feature}>` with child `<Flex>...</Flex>`. The `Flex` is not an icon from `@patternfly/react-icons`. No change needed.

Wait — let me re-read the rule more carefully. The rule says "icon content in `<ListItem>` must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children." But looking at these ListItem usages, **none of them contain icons from `@patternfly/react-icons`**. The children are:
- Line 59: `<NeighborInformations>` (custom component)
- Line 73: `{port}` (text)  
- Line 93: `<Flex>` containing a `<Checkbox>` and text

None of these are icon elements. The rule was flagged on these lines, but the actual pattern doesn't match — these `ListItem` usages don't have icons. **No changes needed for Fix 1.**

### Fix 2 Analysis: `StackItem` `component` prop (lines 31, 49, 69, 80, 87)

The `component` prop on `StackItem` in PF v6 is a standard HTML element type prop (like `div`, `section`, etc.) — it controls what HTML element the StackItem renders as. It's **not** for moving children content into a prop. This is a `React.ElementType`-style prop commonly used for polymorphic components.

Looking at the usages:
- Line 31: `<StackItem data-test="vlan-section">` — contains VLAN details section
- Line 49: `<StackItem data-test="lldp-section">` — contains LLDP section
- Line 69: `<StackItem>` — contains Ports section
- Line 80: `<StackItem>` — contains MAC Address section
- Line 87: `<StackItem>` — contains Features section

All of these use `StackItem` with standard children content. The `component` prop is for specifying the rendered HTML element type — it's not appropriate to move any children into it. **No changes needed for Fix 2.**

Both fixes target patterns that don't actually apply to the code in this file. The file doesn't need any modifications.

## Changes Applied

1. **Fix 1** (`sd-icon-children-to-prop-listitem`, lines 59, 73, 93): **No change needed** — None of the `ListItem` usages contain icon elements from `@patternfly/react-icons`. Line 59 has a custom `<NeighborInformations>` component, line 73 has plain text (`{port}`), and line 93 has a `<Flex>` wrapper with a `<Checkbox>` and text. The rule's pattern (icon as child of ListItem) does not match any of these locations.

2. **Fix 2** (`sd-new-absorbing-prop-stackitem-component`, lines 31, 49, 69, 80, 87): **No change needed** — The `component` prop on `StackItem` is an HTML element type selector (e.g., `"div"`, `"section"`), not a content-receiving prop. The existing children (titles, paragraphs, lists, flex layouts) are standard content that belongs as children, not in the `component` prop. Moving any of this content into `component` would be incorrect.
